summary: Re-linked Runtime/MSL-related objects by moving exact assembly for Gecko_setjmp and MSL trigf out of C inline-asm MUST_MATCH blocks into standalone asm files, updating object lists and configure status, and making a small split-boundary/config correction. Gecko_ExceptionPPC was made matching with an explicit peephole-off pragma rather than a dummy asm function. The PR had no body or review discussion, so intent is inferred from title and diff.
what_changed: Added asm/MSL/trigf.s containing the assembly for tanf, cos__Ff, sin__Ff, cosf, sinf, __sinit_trigf_c, and associated rodata/data/sdata2 objects such as tmp_float, __four_over_pi_m1, __two_over_pi, __SQRT_FLT_EPSILON__, and MSL_TrigF_* labels.
Removed large MUST_MATCH inline-asm blocks for cosf, sinf, and __sinit_trigf_c from src/MSL/trigf.c, leaving the C implementation/stubs and changing exposed float prototypes from f32 to float.
Added asm/Runtime/Gecko_setjmp.s with exact assembly for __setjmp and longjmp, including saving/restoring LR, CR, stack pointer, r2, r13-r31, f14-f31, and FPSCR.
Removed the MUST_MATCH inline assembly implementations of __setjmp and longjmp from src/Runtime/Gecko_setjmp.c, leaving NOT_IMPLEMENTED C bodies while the build links the asm object.
Updated obj_files.mk to link Gecko_setjmp from asm/Runtime/Gecko_setjmp.s.o and trigf from asm/MSL/trigf.s.o instead of their src C objects.

Updated configure.py object statuses: Runtime/Gecko_ExceptionPPC.c became Matching, Runtime/Gecko_setjmp.c became NonMatching, MSL/trigf.c became NonMatching, MSL/buffer_io.c/strtoul.c/console_io.c became Matching, and some Unknown entries were reclassified NonMatching.
configure.py now passes fix_epilogue=False for RuntimeLib.
src/Runtime/Gecko_ExceptionPPC.c replaced an unused static asm function used to trigger a peephole-off bug with an explicit #pragma peephole off.
config/GALE01/splits.txt adjusted MSL/buffer_io.c text end from 0x80323560 to 0x8032355C.
smart_moves: Separated exact assembly from C source by using standalone asm files for objects that are not yet matched from C, keeping the build linkable while simplifying the C files.
Preserved the decompiled C versions of trigf and Gecko_setjmp as NonMatching source rather than deleting them entirely, leaving future matching work visible and localized.
Replaced a dummy unused asm function whose only purpose was affecting compiler peephole behavior with the clearer and more direct #pragma peephole off.
Updated both configure.py object classification and obj_files.mk object paths together, avoiding a mismatch between status metadata and the actual linked object.
Carried over data-section layout details into asm/MSL/trigf.s, including .rodata, .data, .sdata2, .balign 8, SDA labels, and a hidden gap symbol, which helps preserve link/layout behavior.
decomp_lessons: When inline asm under MUST_MATCH dominates a C file, moving it to asm/<module>/<file>.s can make the source file cleaner while preserving exact codegen and enabling a clear NonMatching status for the C implementation.
Object re-link PRs must update every layer that selects objects: configure.py status, obj_files.mk paths, and splits.txt if boundaries shift.
Prefer explicit compiler pragmas over placeholder/dummy asm tricks when the diff shows the only purpose is to affect compiler behavior.
For MSL/runtime work, data placement can matter as much as text: constants and globals may need exact .rodata/.data/.sdata2 layout and alignment in the asm file.

Runtime context-switch functions like setjmp/longjmp are especially sensitive to register/FPSCR save order and offsets; standalone asm is a reasonable holding tactic until exact C/asm constraints are understood.
Small split-boundary corrections can accompany relinks; check adjacent objects like buffer_io.c after changing Runtime/MSL object selection.
naming_conventions: C++-mangled wrapper names appear in C/asm as cos__Ff and sin__Ff for float overload wrappers around cosf/sinf.
MSL anonymous or linker-generated data labels use project address-style names such as MSL_TrigF_80400770, MSL_TrigF_804DE19C, and MSL_TrigF_804DE1A8.
Runtime assembly functions use global .fn/.endfn blocks matching exported names such as __setjmp and longjmp.
Gap symbols may be named with address and section context, e.g. gap_11_804DE1A4_sdata2, and marked .hidden when they only preserve layout.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use standalone asm files with .include "macros.inc", .file, .text, .fn/.endfn, and explicit section declarations for objects that are exact in asm but not matched in C.
Use asm object paths in obj_files.mk for NonMatching C files that should not be linked from their source implementation.
For small-data constants, use .section .sdata2, "a" with .balign 8 and sda21 relocations in the assembly.
The trigf assembly preserves floating-point polynomial/range-reduction behavior with fmadds/fnmadds/fnmsubs sequences and exact constants such as __two_over_pi and __SQRT_FLT_EPSILON__.
The setjmp assembly directly saves/restores machine state with mflr/mfcr/mtlr/mtcrf, stmw/lmw, mffs/mtfsf, and paired stfd/lfd for f14-f31.
Setting fix_epilogue=False for RuntimeLib is part of the relink configuration in this PR; future Runtime relinks should check whether epilogue fixing changes matching/layout.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the PR slice; no reviewer feedback can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that match src/MSL/trigf.c from C after this asm extraction.
Look for later PRs that replace asm/Runtime/Gecko_setjmp.s with matching C or improved declarations for jmp_buf layout.
Search for other uses of #pragma peephole off replacing dummy asm peephole triggers.
Audit RuntimeLib fix_epilogue=False interactions with other Runtime and MetroTRK relink changes.
Check whether the buffer_io.c split end correction affected adjacent MSL/ctype.c boundaries.
